neighbors.name: Military Brothers Moving LLC
neighbors.claims: Military Brothers Moving, LLC is an Arizona moving services company founded in 2014 by two veteran brothers, Kevin and Bryan Hoey [Military Brothers Moving](https://www.militarybrothersmoving.com/) [Better Business Bureau](https://www.bbb.org/us/az/sierra-vista/profile/moving-companies/military-brothers-moving-llc-1286-20087490). The company’s base of operations is at 1301 Cochise Ave in Huachuca City, Arizona, indicating its local presence in the community [MapQuest](https://www.mapquest.com/us/arizona/military-brothers-moving-llc-371175925). Public records show the business categorized purely as a household/commercial moving provider, with no involvement in land development or real estate ventures [Better Business Bureau](https://www.bbb.org/us/az/sierra-vista/profile/moving-companies/military-brothers-moving-llc-1286-20087490). No filings or public statements were found linking this LLC to any stance or activism regarding local development projects (it appears focused on its moving services).
neighbors.influence_justification: Small local service business; no known political ties or large property holdings

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage owners Kevin and Bryan Hoey with friendly outreach emphasizing how the development could bring business (e.g. moving/storage needs). Given their limited stake and lack of opposition history, maintain good relations but intensive engagement is likely unnecessary.

neighbors.name: Kendall Family Trust
neighbors.influence_justification: Private family land trust; influence and stance not publicly documented

overview_summary: The identified neighboring entities consist of a small veteran-owned moving services LLC and a private family land trust. Neither appears to wield significant influence over local development – the moving company is a local business with limited political footprint, and the family trust represents an individual landholding. No major corporate developers, speculative investors, or organized opposition groups are evident among these neighbors, suggesting a low risk of well-funded or orchestrated opposition.
